title: Cleisostoma inflatum
intro: Cleisostoma inflatum is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and boosting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Cleisostoma inflatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cleisostoma inflatum is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the form of dried roots and rhizomes to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Cleisostoma inflatum is a species of orchid native to the Himalayas. It belongs to the genus Cleisostoma. This genus is part of the subtribe Aeridinae, which is within the family Orchidaceae. The subfamily Orchidoideae is also a part of the Orchidaceae family and is a superfamily to Aeridinae. Cleisostoma inflatum is classified as an orchid species within the flowering plant kingdom.
